Filter comparison summary:
  Gaussian  – Laplacian var=0.77, MAD=0.6389
  Median    – Laplacian var=1.19, MAD=0.6644
  Bilateral – Laplacian var=0.70, MAD=0.6961

Best edge-preserving filter: 'median'
Justification:
  • Gaussian: fast, isotropic smoothing. Blurs both noise AND edges equally.
  • Median:   non-linear; excellent at salt-and-pepper noise without blurring.
  • Bilateral: edge-preserving; smooths homogeneous regions while keeping     boundaries sharp. Best for seed outlines but may be slower.
  → 'median' retains the most edge information (highest Laplacian variance), making it the preferred choice for seed boundary detection.
